observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misclassification
of the assets and the overstatement of the accumulated depreciation
account; and

b. Ensure that an item of property, plant, and equipment shall only be
recognized in the books of accounts in accordance with IPSAS 45.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Direct the officials concerned to fast track the processing of the deed of
donations of the two emergency vehicles and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant for the dropping of the properties from the books
of account;

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property,
Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and ensure that they are reconciled
with the property records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer; and

c. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Inventory Committee to conduct
physical count of all PPE, including the fixed assets and medical
equipment of the Municipality, and report the same in the Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
misclassification of the 20% LGU share from Fire Code Fees from the Due to
NGAs account to the Trust Liabilities account.

We recommended that Management:

a. Demand from the Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of the
three barangays with outstanding CY 2023 financial support amounting
to ₱75,000.00 the submission of liquidation reports and documents
thereof;

b. Monitor and regularly coordinate with the barangays to ensure that all
liquidation reports are submitted on or before December of each year and
recorded in the correct accounting year; and

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify and record all liquidation
reports received to arrive at the correct balance of the Due from LGUs
account.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Require the Supply Officer to immediately submit the SSMI and RIS for
inventories issued amounting to ₱308,801.26 to the MAccO;

b. Require the MAccO to prepare the correcting journal entries to fix the
balances of the Inventory, Expenses and Accounts Payable accounts;

c. Require the MAccO and Supply Officer to reconcile the RPCI and other
property records with the books of accounts and to make the necessary
adjustments or corrections; and

d. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the Subsidiary
Ledgers of Welfare Goods for Distribution account.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Demand the accountable officers to execute a certificate of justification
as to why the supporting documents are not available in his/her office or
any other office in accordance with item 7.1 (f)(i) of COA Circular No.
2023-008 dated August 17, 2023;

b. Exert all efforts to collect the receivables from the persons responsible.
If all efforts are futile, file the necessary request for write-off of dormant
receivables that are deemed uncollectible, supported with complete
documentation as listed in Annex 8 of COA Circular no. 2023-008,
provided with index tabs, for easy reference; and

c. Evaluate the significant increase of credit risk and its equivalent amount
to recognize the loss allowance of the receivable to ensure that they are
fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to revert the long outstanding
payables to the unappropriated balance of the fund against which no
actual claims, administrative or judicial, have been filed or which are not
covered by perfected contracts on record; and

b. Advice the Budget Officer to certify only obligations funded on a valid
claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Submit immediately the bank reconciliation statement as of December
31, 2024 for the General Fund together with the statement of accounts
and all supporting documents to the audit team; and

b. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the reversal of stale
checks to correct the balances of the Cash in Bank – LCCA and Accounts
Payable accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure prompt submission of all
reports of collections and deposits and its supporting documents to the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that the said financial transactions are
recorded in the correct accounting period;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the correcting journal entry
to correct the misstatements of the Due from Other Funds and the
Interest Income accounts caused by the error in recording in the book of
accounts of the General Fund of the interest earned in the 1st quarter of
CY 2024 on bank deposits under the Trust Fund; and

c. Monitor the intra-agency transactions and ensure the reconciliation and
elimination of intra-agency accounts in the consolidated financial
statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately correct her
cashbooks and to ensure that the cashbooks are updated and balanced
daily; and

b. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile their records monthly or at least quarterly and to make the
necessary adjustments to ensure accurate cash balances.

We recommended Management to direct the:

a. Property officer to update the IIRUP to reflect the correct details of the
unserviceable properties and to include the three unserviceable
properties amounting to ₱103,214.00; and

b. Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the
unserviceable assets in accordance with the specific guidelines set in
the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties
prescribed by the COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 dated January 30,
2024 to avoid further deterioration and to generate income from their
sales.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Council to:

a. Evaluate and identify PPAs that are feasible for the current year and
update the LDRRMF Investment plan to include the identified activities to
be funded from the unutilized LDRRMF;

b. Review thoroughly the LDRRMF Investment Plan to ascertain that the
disaster risk management activities are not overfunded and identical with
the activities sourced out from other funds; and

c. Closely monitor the execution of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely
implemented.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the revision or update
of the AIP or the preparation of a supplemental AIP whenever changes arise
to be submitted to the Local Budget Officer and approved by the
Sanggunian, before the procurement and implementation of any programs,
projects and activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the GSIS Agency
Authorized Officer and Electronic Remittance File Officer to regularly update
membership profiles of member-employees and ensure that reconciliation and
billing issues are resolved before processing the following month’s remittance.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to
comply with laws, rules and regulations to avoid audit suspensions,
disallowances, and charges and settle the same within the prescribed period
to prevent their accumulation.

1.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting journal
entries and recognize in the books of accounts as part of inventories
the identified semi-expendable items in accordance with the Pro-
forma entries as illustrated in COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated
March 14, 2024; and
b. The Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of semi-
expendable items with due consideration of the issuance of the GSO
of the Inventory Custodianship Slip to establish accountability of
the end-user by strictly observing the prescribed threshold amount
and properly disclosing in the Notes to FS all details relating to
semi-expendable items.

2.11 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant provide the necessary adjusting records
to reclassify the completed projects and provide appropriate
depreciation charges;
b. The Municipal Accountant adhere to generally accepted accounting
principles by properly recognizing the prepayments from the advance
mobilization fees paid to contractors and provide adjusting journal
entries for the ₱806,790.34 recorded as CIP;
c. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accounting Office
with a quarterly copy of completed projects to update records;
d. The Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to FS any
movements of CIP and PPE to ensure accuracy and fairness in the
presentation of the FS; and
e. The Municipal accountant include in the closing instructions before
closing the books at year-end, all the necessary procedures to be
undertaken by the different offices to ensure complete and accurate
financial reports at each reporting date.

3.7 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant record the necessary adjusting entries
and correctly reclassify the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account;
b. The Municipal Accountant refer to the prescribed chart of accounts
per COA Circular No. 2015-09 dated December 1, 2015, and use the
appropriate account titles in recording transactions;
c. The Municipal Accountant disclose all pertinent details of the TL-
DRRMF in the Notes to the FS to enable readers and users of the FS
to better understand its contents; and
d. The Municipal accountant includes in the closing instructions
before closing the books at year-end, all the necessary procedures
to be undertaken by the different offices to ensure complete and
accurate financial reports at each reporting date.

4.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant submit an explanation on the variance or
discrepancy between the Cash in Bank Balances as presented in the
Financial Statements and the Bank Reconciliation Statement; and
b. Reconcile the cash records and prepare necessary adjusting entries
in the Cash in Bank Account.

5.8 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting journal entry at every
year-end for any unreleased/unclaimed checks issued restoring the
Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account, and the
recognition/restoration of liabilities account to faithfully present the
financial position of the Municipality as at December 31 of any given
year; and
b. Proper coordination between the offices of the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer for the list of unreleased checks as of the
cutoff date to facilitate accurate preparation of journal entry vouchers
and ensure proper recording of the same.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer hasten the
reconciliation of Cash in Bank records to bring the account to its
correct amount. Thereafter, conduct reconciliation monthly; and
b. Ensure that both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant have a uniform application of the standards in
recognizing and recording cash transactions.

7.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant conduct regular monitoring and review of
accounts to evaluate the current collectability status and provide for
an allowance for impairment of receivables; and
b. The Office of the Mayor implement strategies to implement the
proper disposition of dormant receivables in strict compliance with
the procedures and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005, as
amended by COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

8.24 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The GSO prepare a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) supported with the corresponding Requisitions and Issued
Slips (RIS) to account for the issuances of office supplies, and as
basis of the MAO to prepare the JEV to record the supplies issued
during the period;
b. The requisitioning units/ concerned offices issued with inventories
maintain a stock cards per stock number and prepare an inventory
report every semester to serve as a reference for the GSO during
their conduct of actual physical count of inventories at each
reporting date;
c. Construct or provide an inventory stock room to enhance the GSO’s
storage and recordkeeping capabilities;
d. The Municipal Accounting Office and the GSO maintain the Supply
Ledger Cards to reconcile with the Stock Cards, respectively, to
provide a system of check and balance in the recording of
inventories in accordance with the perpetual inventory system, in
keeping with the accounting policies prescribed in the pertinent
provisions of the NGAS Manual, Volume I;
e. The GSO submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)
to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 124 of NGAS
and reconcile the reported inventories in the FS and the physical
inventories on hand including the non-moving items; after which,
prepare adjusting journal entries, if warranted;
f. The Municipal Accountant to review the observed variances
between the subsidiary ledgers and the reported FS; and
g. Provide adequate disclosures as prescribed under Paragraph 47 of
IPSAS 12 to enhance understanding of the users of the financial
statements.

9.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of
the year with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and
the amount due and collectible for the year, to serve as the basis on
the recording of the Real Property Tax Receivables/Special
Education Tax Receivables and crediting to Deferred Real Property
Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income in accordance
with Section 20 of the NGAS for LGUs Volume I;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the Abstract of Real Property
Tax/ Collections to facilitate the distribution and remittance of the
shares of the different government units concerned in the real
property tax collections pursuant to Sections 34 of the NGAS for
LGUs Volume I.
c. The MTO provide relevant data regarding advance receipts and
discount on prompt and advance payments to the MAO to recognize
the collection of Advance Receipts at its gross amount and the
discount to Discount on Advance Payment in compliance with
Section 22 of the NGAS for LGUs Volume I and pertinent provisions
of IPSAS 23;
d. The Municipal Accountant provide adequate disclosures regarding
sudden increases and decreases of account balances to provide
additional information and value for a better understanding of
accounting data as presented in the Financial Statements at year-
end; and
e. The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office for the reports/ documents necessary to support
the adjustment to the books regarding the noted errors/ deficiencies
and correct the understatement/ overstatement of the affected
accounts in the financial statements as presented in the table above.

10.11 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant conduct immediate and proper
validation of real properties based on the inventory report of the
Assessor’s Office and establish actual ownership of the land and
that future economic benefits will flow to the Municipality in line
with IPSAS 17;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries
to record all lands owned by the Municipality, in consonance with
Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public
Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1; and
c. The Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Real Property
Ledger Cards with complete details as to its description, location,
acquisition cost, additions and disposition that will serve as a
subsidiary ledger to support the balances reported in the SFP and
cross balance with the report of the assessor’s office.

11.14 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant immediately conduct reconciliation of
records with the GSOs so that the accuracy of the reported PPE
will not be compromised;
b. The members of the inventory committee prioritize the completion
of one-time PPE cleansing and require the Municipal Accountant
to effect the necessary adjusting entries once it is accomplished;
c. The Municipal accountant to include in the closing instructions
before closing the books at year-end, all the necessary procedures
to be undertaken by the different offices to ensure complete and
accurate financial reports at each reporting date; and
d. The Municipal accountant provides adequate disclosures in the
Notes to FS regarding PPE.

12.8 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant ensure proper review of the FS
submitted to be free from material misstatements so that its
readers and users have a clear grasp of the Municipality’s
operations; and
b. Provide all line items with adequate disclosure in compliance with
Paragraph 127 (c) of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2016-004, to
provide additional information and value for a better
understanding of accounting data as presented in the Financial
Statements at year-end.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Assessor secure a copy of the land documents
such as Deed of Sale, Deed of Donation or other supporting
documents in the Provincial Assessor’s Office for those
parcels of land which mode of acquisition and equivalent
cost were not yet determined. Submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant as her bases in the recording of
assets in the book. Likewise, fast track the land titling
processes to prevent from disputes and fraud; and
b. Municipal Accountant draw journal entry to record the land
with amounts based on the purchase price at the time of
sale and fair value at the time of donation.

We recommended that the:
a.) Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the designated
Municipal General Service Officer, exert more efforts in
securing second copies of other supporting documents

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to restate the balance of accounts affected by adjustments
made due to prior period errors and disclose in the NFS the nature and
impact of the errors.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Budget and Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile their records to come
up with a reliable SCBAA.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to disclose in the NFS
the explanation of material differences between the final budget and actual
amounts and reconciliation of actual amounts with the financial statements
for a fair presentation of the SCBAA.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Record the remaining infrastructure assets in the books of accounts
using their respective replacement costs;
b. Determine with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer, the remaining
useful life of the unrecorded assets and provide for the corresponding
accumulated depreciation; and
c. Adhere to IPSAS 17 for the fair presentation of infrastructure assets in
the financial statements.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the
misclassification of the asset and the overstatement of depreciation
account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misclassification
of the heavy equipment and adjust the balance of the PPE based on the
reconciled reports; and
b. Prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card for
each category of assets; Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and Local
Road Network Ledger Card for local road networks segregated by
components.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Inventory Committee to conduct complete count of PPE accounts, prepare
the RPCPPPE and RPCLRN and subsequently reconcile these reports with
the records maintained by the Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal
Treasurer and Property Officer.

We further recommended and the Management agreed to consider the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts to establish PPE balances that are
verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records and make the
necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the five per cent
DPWH share and the 15 per cent OBO share.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
following journal entry to record the CY 2023 15 per cent OBO share, once
established:
Due from Other Funds xxx
Trust Liabilities xxx

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
a. Supply Officer to immediately submit the SSMI and RIS to support the
issuance of inventory items amounting to ₱1,345,486.08;
b. Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to reconcile their respective
records on inventories.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accounting Office to:
a. Draw the necessary journal entry vouchers to arrive at the correct
balance of the various Inventory and Expense accounts; and
b. Prepare the subsidiary ledger for the Other Supplies and Materials for
Distribution, if this inventory is still existing, otherwise, prepare an
adjusting entry to correct the balance of the account.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to consider the
following journal entry to recognize the Prepaid Insurance, after adjusting
for the expired portion in CY 2024:
Prepaid Insurance xxx
Prior Period Adjustment xxx

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
henceforth prepare year-end adjusting entries to record the unexpended
portion of the insurance premium.

We recommended and the Management agreed to request the write-off of
dormant accounts accompanied by the supporting documents listed in
Annex 8 of COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Provide Allowance for Impairment to present the receivables account in
their net realizable value;
b. Prepare the subsidiary ledgers for the RPT Receivable, SET Receivable,
and Receivables-Disallowances and Charges; and
c. Comply with the measures provided in the guidelines for documents
that are absent, lost, destroyed, or cannot be produced.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to monitor intra-agency transactions and to reconcile the
reciprocal accounts, and cause the elimination thereof in the consolidated
financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to record all cash transactions
in her cashbook daily; and
b. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly and make the
necessary adjustments to ensure accurate cash balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to determine the current fair
value of the ambulance and thereafter direct the Municipal Accountant to
recognize the asset in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the BAC
personnel concerned to refund the amount of ₱58,923.68 and ensure that
succeeding payment of honoraria come from the sources enumerated
under Item 3.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the
misclassification of the collection of liquidated damages as trust liabilities.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count and
update its RPCPPE to include all insurable properties;
b. Cause the appraisal of all its insurable properties and other assets;
c. Immediately prepare and submit the PIF to the audit team and GSIS, not
later than April 30 of each year; and
d. Ensure timely insurance renewal to avoid gap in the coverage and
potential losses due to non-indemnification in case of untoward incidents.

We recommended and the Municipal Development Council agreed to:
a. Fast track the identification of the local development and livelihood
projects to be charged out of the National Wealth Fund as soon as it
becomes available;
b. Consider expanding the scope of livelihood projects beyond solely
providing loans to enhance the utilization of National Wealth Fund in
compliance with relevant guidelines; and
c. Require the Municipal Engineering Office to strictly monitor and ensure
the timely completion of the projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the MDRRMC and
MDRRMO to:
a. Ensure that only fully funded disaster risk management activities are
included in the LDRRMFIP;
b. Identify the disaster risk management activities to be charged out of
LDRRMF amounting to ₱173,848.00;
c. Review thoroughly the LDRRMFIP to ascertain that the disaster risk
management activities are not overfunded and identical with the
activities source out from other funds;
d. Discontinue disaster risk management activities that are no longer
economically, financially, or technically feasible and subsequently
reprogram the available fund to other priority disaster risk
management activities.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to require the BAC to
ensure that only procurement ready disaster risk management activities are
included in the Annual Procurement Plan.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the MDRRMO to
coordinate with the Municipal Planning Development Office to ensure full
incorporation of the LDRRMFIP in the Annual/Supplemental Investment
Program.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the BAC to:
a. Conduct early procurement to facilitate immediate implementation of
the infrastructure projects; and
b. Ensure that right-of-way site or location were acquired or secured and
detailed engineering investigations were sufficiently carried out before
awarding the infrastructure projects to avoid unnecessary suspension or
termination of the contract.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with
the calibrated actions in response to delays in the implementation of the
infrastructure projects to ensure their timely completion.

We further recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Municipal Development Council and BAC to ensure that only well-planned
and procurement-and-implementation ready projects are incorporated in
the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Procurement Plan.

We recommended and the Management agreed to confer with the
government official concerned to clarify the conditions set in the Deed of
Donation and to inform him of the marking requirements and prohibitions
spelled out in COA Circular No. 77-61 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2010-101. If the donor consents to the removal of the prohibited markings,
request that the Deed of Donation to be revise to such effect. Otherwise,
return the donation in view of conflict with the aforesaid rules and
regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Acting
Municipal Treasurer, upon reconciliation, to immediately remit to the
Bureau of Treasury the five per cent share of the DPWH and to regularly
monitor collections of income from building permits to ensure timely
remittance of the DPWH share.

We recommended and the Gender and Development Focal Point System
(GFPS) agreed to estimate the cost of each GAD activity by object of
expenditure for a more realistic budget and promote efficient use of the
GAD fund.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the Cash in Bank Account in order to present reliable balance.

We recommended that for PPE items with acquisition cost of P50,000.00 and below, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting entries to reclassify the affected PPE in accordance with COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022, with due consideration of the issuance of the Inventory Custodianship Slip to establish accountability of the end-user.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Prioritize the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit it to this office for verification.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cashbooks.

We recommended that the Management prioritize the utilization of the LDRRMF prior years’ unexpended balance. Further, revert back to the general fund any unutilized amount after five years to be programmed to other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to continue the preparation and maintenance of a detailed ledger card for each class of PPE in soft copies, review the composition and balances of each PPE item to reconcile the noted variance of ₱(80,490,895.15) between the balances per supporting schedule and as presented in the Statement of Financial Position (SFP) to comply with Section 114(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office in coordination with the members of the Inventory Committee to fast-track the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and ensure the immediate and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the Municipality’s PPEs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023 the required details/information for the Property, Plant and Equipment including Total Road Network System in accordance with IPSAS provisions and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile their records on inventories and properties to reflect its correct balances in the books of accounts;
Require the GSO to prepare inventory reports and the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records of the asset accounts mentioned; and
Looking forward, provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to FS to better assist users of the financial statements in understanding its contents.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant, GSO Designate, and the Municipal Assessor to evaluate their records so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17 for a fair presentation of the land account in the Statement of Financial Position at year-end.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Revisit the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-02 regarding the accounting journal entries on LDRRMF;
Provide adjusting journal entries to properly recognize unexpended LDRRMF by crediting the account Trust Liabilities – LDRRMF; and
Adhere to IPSAS 35 and Section 111 of PD 1445 by ensuring that the reciprocal accounts are properly reconciled.

We recommended that the Management:
Looking forward, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the necessary subsidiary records as required under Section 114(2) of PD 1445, provide the details of the Accounts Payable (source documents, payee, nature of liability) and the aging of AP, especially at year-end, and submit supporting documents in the recognition of AP to complete the evaluation and to enable us to decide in the audit; and
Exhaust all means in determining whether the said payable is still due and demandable. Otherwise, revert the balance of accounts payable to the unappropriated general fund according to the provisions of PD 1445 to fairly present the liability accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit justifications explaining how the amounts intended for the urinals and ceramic tiles were spent and what are the reasons behind the changes which shall be duly noted and approved by the Municipal Mayor so that the audit team can forward the same to the Technical Services Office for reconsideration;
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to enhance its monitoring and quality assurance mechanisms to prevent future non-compliance issues. The LGU should implement stricter oversight, inspections, and reporting procedures for ongoing and future projects; and
Direct the BAC to review the contract terms to identify the specific provisions related to non-compliance and enforce the appropriate contractual remedies. Hold the contractor accountable for the deficiencies, which may involve contractual penalties or other specified actions.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the timely submission of disbursement vouchers for the grant of cash advance for CF with complete supporting documents as prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular 2015-01.

We recommended that Management:
Provide justifications why the elected official was allowed to travel abroad despite the LGU having no budget for foreign travel; and
Looking forward, strictly adhere to the provisions on budgeting and principles of disbursements regarding the payment of expenditures by ensuring that a line item for foreign travel is included in the approved annual budget.

We recommended that the Management refrain from utilizing the LDRRMF for non-disaster-related programs, projects, and/or activities in conformity with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 to ensure proper fund utilization, accurate reporting and attain the purpose for which the fund was intended.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of directly procuring goods to a supplier through MOA. Instead, conduct a public bidding and enter into a framework agreement to ensure efficiency in procurement is attained. Further, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR.

We recommended that Management procure through public bidding or resort to an appropriate alternative mode of procurement by strictly adhering to RA 9184 and its IRR rather than resorting to shopping when the amount of each transaction exceeds the threshold.

We recommended that Management:
Revisit the municipal gym’s master plan for any design revisions to implement the project in phases considering LGU's immediate needs and available budget to implement the renovation of the municipal gym through competitive bidding and avoid incurring unplanned expenditures that resulted in the splitting of purchases; and
Require the BAC to strictly monitor the LGU’s procurement to avoid the incurrence of another splitting of contracts.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the MHO to submit justification to prove reasonableness for the procurement of the afore-cited items above the DPRI of the DOH;
Looking forward, strengthen the internal controls placed in the procurement of medicines by directing the MHO to coordinate with the GSO to take into consideration the latest updates on the drug price references index during the preparation of Purchase Requests; and
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to procure drugs and medicines at reasonable prices guided by the applicable DPRI set by DOH in accordance with DOH Administrative Order No. 2019-0040 dated October 14, 2019, to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the SORD, especially at year-end for accurate reporting of fund utilization, transparency, and accountability reasons;
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit for contract review the procurement of goods related to the Supplemental Feeding Program following Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and
Looking forward, strictly adhere to the terms and conditions enumerated in the MOA for fund transfers.

2.7	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable to record the 10 percent Retention Fee and prepare adjusting entry to reclassify the accounts affected.

3.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the three properties and their related Accumulated Depreciation to Equity account and thereafter to observe the ₽50,000.00 capitalization threshold as required in Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

4.8	We recommended that the Municipal Services Officer maintain the required Property Cards for each class of PPE as provided under Section 114 of MNGAS, LGU to facilitate the reconciliation of any noted discrepancy between the records of the Accounting and the General Services Office.

The concerned Offices continue exerting collaborative efforts for the realization of the One Time Cleansing of the PPE account of the Municipality by undertaking the steps provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

6.8	We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, the savings from completed projects of the 20 percent Development Fund in the amount of P197,986.55 upon the recommendation of the Local Chief Executive, and the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

7.7	We recommended the LDRRMO ensure the submission of the updated LDRRMP/LDRRMFIP to the MPD Officer for appropriate consideration of the PPAs from the reprogrammed prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in the AIP and eventual inclusion of the same in the annual appropriation and monitoring of the Budget Office.

8.6	We recommended that the LDRRMO refrain from identifying, processing and charging administrative costs from the LDRRMF, and the Management through the Local Chief Executive, the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer and Budget Officer, disapprove payment of the same.

9.8	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant, with the approval of the local Sanggunian, open a separate depository account for the current DRRMF and transfer the allocated fund therein regularly.  Moreover, maintain a separate filing of the corresponding DVs and RCI for easy monitoring of its disbursements.

10.6	We recommended that the implementing office of the projects submit the updated PPMP to the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the APP all procurements to be undertaken during the year and ensure that any changes thereof will be effected and submitted to the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in accordance with Section 7.4 of the same R.A.

a.	The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant prioritize the opening of a separate trust fund account to transfer the unexpended LDRRM funds for ease of monitoring; and

b.	The Municipal Accountant prepares adjusting entries to recognize the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account for the unutilized DRRMF in the trust fund books.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accounting Office to consider the following adjusting entry for the stale checks to reflect the correct balance of Cash in Bank – LCCA and Accounts Payable accounts:
Accounts Debit Credit
Cash In Bank – LCCA 358,701.44
Accounts Payable 349,792.02
Due to LGUs 8,909.42
1.11. We also recommended and management agreed to:
a. follow up in writing the request for copies of debit memoranda in the
amount of ₱17,400.00 to serve as basis in drawing the appropriate
adjusting entries;
b. inform the bank of the reconciling items amounting to ₱5,984.18 and
provide a copy of the Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly for their
appropriate action
1.12. We further recommended and the MAccO agreed to, henceforth, ensure
that all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and corrections of
the books of accounts are immediately recorded.

2.10. We recommended and management agreed to require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers showing
the amount of tax due and collectibles for the year and submit the same
to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the recognition of Real
Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the
beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of the MNGAS.
b. Municipal Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers of all
taxpayers; and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records/reports at year end to bring the RPT Receivable and SET
Receivable accounts to their correct balances.
2.11. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant consider the
possibility of installing the Electronic National Government
Accounting System or the e-NGAS to automate the recognition of
government financial transactions and generation of the LGU’s
financial reports.

3.9 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. send another demand letter to the concerned NGOs/POs for the
immediate submission of the Fund Utilization Report;
b. conduct close monitoring and proper evaluation of the projects
undertaken by the recipient NGOs/POs to ensure that funds
transferred were utilized for its intended purpose and regularly submit
monitoring reports to the office of the Municipal Mayor copy furnish
the office of the Sangguniang Bayan Office as stated in the
Memorandum of Agreements; and
c. avail of legal remedies in the event of continuous non-compliance by the
POs/NGOs of the requirement on the submission of liquidation reports.

4.11 We recommended and management agreed to require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on inventory accounts
and submit the resulting RPCIs every semester;
b. Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile their records with the RPCIs
and draw accounting entries to record the issuance and consumption
of the supplies and materials; and
c. Property Officer to maintain stock cards for all inventory items to
facilitate reconciliation of records with the Accounting Office.

5.7 We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to initially recognize all purchases of goods for
stockpiling as inventory and subsequently recognize them as expense upon
issuance to the intended users/beneficiaries to ensure that balances of
inventory accounts are accurate and reliable.

6.8. We recommended and the management agreed to direct the:
a. MAccO to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for all the PPEs
of the Municipality;
b. MAccO to regularly prepare a detailed lapsing report of the
Municipality’s PPEs to support the recording of the Depreciation
Expenses and Accumulated Depreciation accounts of the PPEs; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and the Inventory Committee to ensure that physical
count of PPEs and its resulting RPCPPE are complete for the subsequent
years.
6.9. We further recommended and the management agreed to consider the
installation of the e-NGAS to facilitate the proper recording of the PPE
accounts, their pertinent depreciation and accumulated depreciation
accounts and their carrying values.

7.11 We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal
Engineer to submit the complete description and cost segregation of road
components of every road network, as well as the total network system, to
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as basis in their
reporting and disclosure in the Notes to FS.
7.12 We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to thereafter,
through the Inventory Committee, prepare and submit the Report on the
Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road
Network.
7.13 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. establish and maintain Local Network Ledger Cards for the road and
its components;
b. provide depreciation by road component after determination of the
cost of each component and maintain the necessary subsidiary
ledgers, taking into consideration the guidelines set in Part V of COA
Circular No. 2015-008;
c. prepare a lapsing schedule on the road networks regularly as basis in
the computation of the correct Depreciation and Accumulated
Depreciation amounts; and
d. draw adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous depreciation
recognized in the books of accounts.

8.7 We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to consider the following proposed adjusting entry to reflect
the correct balance of the affected accounts:
Accounts Debit Credit
Due to Officers and Employees ₱ 577,846.41
Prior Years Adjustment ₱ 577,846.41

Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Engineering
Office to:

b. enhance coordination between officers involved in the
implementation of local development projects; and
c. evaluate the unimplemented projects and determine if still feasible,
otherwise, realign their budgetary allocations to other priority
projects in the Annual Investment Plan.

and response capabilities of the community.

necessity and feasibility of the current proposed projects, otherwise,

officials to:

loss and wastage of government resources and ensure efficient and
effective delivery of relief goods to beneficiaries; and

goods to maintain longer shelf life and to facilitate efficient monitoring
and reporting of the status of the relief goods.
